verses.text: In the wilderness of Sin the people murmur, wishing they had died in Egypt by the fleshpots. Hunger. Want makes them romanticize slavery.
In the wilderness of Sin the people murmur, wishing they had died in Egypt by the fleshpots. Hunger. Want makes them romanticize slavery.
In the wilderness of Sin the people murmur, wishing they had died in Egypt by the fleshpots. Hunger. Want makes them romanticize slavery.
God promises to rain bread from heaven, to be gathered daily as a test. Daily bread. He provides, and proves their trust.
God promises to rain bread from heaven, to be gathered daily as a test. Daily bread. He provides, and proves their trust.
Moses says their murmurings are against the LORD, not against him. Against God. Complaint about His provision is sin against Him.
Moses says their murmurings are against the LORD, not against him. Against God. Complaint about His provision is sin against Him.
Moses says their murmurings are against the LORD, not against him. Against God. Complaint about His provision is sin against Him.
The glory of the LORD appears; He will give flesh at even and bread in the morning. Glory and gift. God answers murmuring with mercy.
The glory of the LORD appears; He will give flesh at even and bread in the morning. Glory and gift. God answers murmuring with mercy.
The glory of the LORD appears; He will give flesh at even and bread in the morning. Glory and gift. God answers murmuring with mercy.
The glory of the LORD appears; He will give flesh at even and bread in the morning. Glory and gift. God answers murmuring with mercy.
Quails cover the camp, and in the morning small round manna; 'It is manna.' What is it? God feeds them with a daily wonder.
Quails cover the camp, and in the morning small round manna; 'It is manna.' What is it? God feeds them with a daily wonder.
Quails cover the camp, and in the morning small round manna; 'It is manna.' What is it? God feeds them with a daily wonder.
Each gathers his fill; 'he that gathered much had nothing over.' Enough. God's supply is measured to need.
Each gathers his fill; 'he that gathered much had nothing over.' Enough. God's supply is measured to need.
Each gathers his fill; 'he that gathered much had nothing over.' Enough. God's supply is measured to need.
Manna left till morning bred worms; it must be gathered fresh each day. Fresh daily. God's mercies are received new each morning.
Manna left till morning bred worms; it must be gathered fresh each day. Fresh daily. God's mercies are received new each morning.
Manna left till morning bred worms; it must be gathered fresh each day. Fresh daily. God's mercies are received new each morning.
On the sixth day they gather double; the seventh is a sabbath rest, with none to find. Sabbath. God provides for rest and honours His day.
On the sixth day they gather double; the seventh is a sabbath rest, with none to find. Sabbath. God provides for rest and honours His day.
On the sixth day they gather double; the seventh is a sabbath rest, with none to find. Sabbath. God provides for rest and honours His day.
On the sixth day they gather double; the seventh is a sabbath rest, with none to find. Sabbath. God provides for rest and honours His day.
On the sixth day they gather double; the seventh is a sabbath rest, with none to find. Sabbath. God provides for rest and honours His day.
Some go out on the sabbath and find nothing; God bids them keep the rest. Disobedience. They must learn to trust the day of rest.
Some go out on the sabbath and find nothing; God bids them keep the rest. Disobedience. They must learn to trust the day of rest.
Some go out on the sabbath and find nothing; God bids them keep the rest. Disobedience. They must learn to trust the day of rest.
Some go out on the sabbath and find nothing; God bids them keep the rest. Disobedience. They must learn to trust the day of rest.
A pot of manna is laid up before the Testimony as a memorial. Remembered. The wonder is stored for later generations.
A pot of manna is laid up before the Testimony as a memorial. Remembered. The wonder is stored for later generations.
A pot of manna is laid up before the Testimony as a memorial. Remembered. The wonder is stored for later generations.
A pot of manna is laid up before the Testimony as a memorial. Remembered. The wonder is stored for later generations.
Israel ate manna forty years, till they came to Canaan's border. Faithful supply. God fed them all the wilderness way.
